tools.name: access.accessAdd
tools.description: Adds/Overwrites the user entry in the access control database.

tools.name: access.accessDel
tools.description: Using '*' for user clears all the users.

tools.name: access.accessGetAcldb
tools.description: If nothing is specified then this returns "".

tools.name: access.accessGetAdmindir
tools.description: If nothing is specified in admin/settings.cfg then returns "". If no admin directory is specified then the shared area will be used where needed (e.g. for persistent info, access control data files etc. )

tools.name: access.accessGetAdminuser
tools.description: If nothing is specified in admin/settings.cfg then returns "".

tools.name: access.accessGetEnabled
tools.description: 0 indicates that it is disabled, 1 indicates it is enabled.

tools.name: access.accessList
tools.description: Each entry consists of user, agents (in minimal range representation) and access mask (not used currently).

tools.name: access.accessLoad
tools.description: If filename is not specified then the currently set 'acldb' parameter is used.

tools.name: access.accessSave
tools.description: If filename is not specified then the currently set 'acldb' parameter is used.

tools.name: access.accessSetAcldb
tools.description: This will be used for subsequent load and save operations.

tools.name: agent.addIpalias
tools.description: port defaults to 161 if not specified. mask defaults to the class-based network mask for the address. interface defaults to the default network interface.  If port is set to 0, the system will automatically select a port number. This is useful for client-mode protocols, such as TFTP or TOD. Upon start of an IP alias with a 0 (auto-assigned) port number, its port will change to contain the value of the selected system port.

tools.name: agent.addTimerScript
tools.description: Add a new timer script to be executed at specified interval (in msec) with the specified argument.

tools.name: agent.agentRemove
tools.description: For speed, this operation will complete asynchronously. The same synchronization considerations apply as in /mimic/agent/start.

tools.name: agent.agentStoreCopy
tools.description: This command copies the variable store from the other agent to this agent.

tools.name: agent.agentStoreExists
tools.description: It returns "1" if the variable exists, else "0".

tools.name: agent.agentStoreGet
tools.description: The value will be returned as a string (like all Tcl values).

tools.name: agent.agentStoreList
tools.description: The list will be a Tcl format list with curly braces "{}" around each list element. These elements in turn are space separated.

tools.name: agent.agentStoreLreplace
tools.description: These commands treat the variable as a list, and allow to replace an entry in the list at the specified index with the specified value. The variable has to already exist.

tools.name: agent.agentStorePersists
tools.description: It returns "1" if the variable is persistent, else "0".

tools.name: agent.agentStoreSet
tools.description: The append sub-command will append the value to an existing variable, or create a new one. The set sub-command will overwrite an existing variable, or create a new one. The optional persist flag can be used to indicate if the variable is to be persistent as described above. By default a value of '0' will be implied for the persist flag. To avoid mistakes, for existing variables the persist flag can only be set. If you want to reset it, you first need to unset the variable.

tools.name: agent.agentStoreUnset
tools.description: This will cleanup persistent variables if needed

tools.name: agent.delIpalias
tools.description: port defaults to 161 if not specified.

tools.name: agent.delTimerScript
tools.description: The first scheduled script that matches the script name, and optionally the interval and argument will be deleted.

tools.name: agent.fromAdd
tools.description: An empty ipaddress or 0.0.0.0 both imply any address. Similarly an empty port or 0 both imply any port. For agents with source-address-indexing enabled, messages which do not match any source address will be discarded with an ERROR message, similar to community string mismatches.

tools.name: agent.fromDel
tools.description: An empty ipaddress or 0.0.0.0 both imply any address. Similarly an empty port or 0 both imply any port. For agents with source-address-indexing enabled, messages which do not match any source address will be discarded with an ERROR message, similar to community string mismatches.

tools.name: agent.fromList
tools.description: This in effect implements source-address-indexing, where 2 agents with the same address can be configured, each accepting messages from different management stations.

tools.name: agent.getChanged
tools.description: has the agent value space changed?

tools.name: agent.getDrops
tools.description: drop rate (every N-th PDU). 0 means no drops.

tools.name: agent.getHost
tools.description: Currently, only IPv4 addresses are allowed as the main address of the agent, but both IPv4 and IPv6 addresses are allowed as IP aliases for the agent.

tools.name: agent.getInformTimeout
tools.description: The agent will retransmit INFORM PDUs at this interval until it has received a reply from the manager.

tools.name: agent.getInterface
tools.description: network interface card for the agent.

tools.name: agent.getMibs
tools.description: set of MIBs, simulations and scenarios

tools.name: agent.getNumberStarts
tools.description: This count is incremented each time an agent starts. It affects the SNMPv3 EngineBoots parameter.

tools.name: agent.getPdusize
tools.description: The limit for this configurable is 65536.

tools.name: agent.getProtocols
tools.description: protocols supported by agent as an array of strings

tools.name: agent.getStatistics
tools.description: The statistics are returned as 64-bit decimal numbers for the following statistics, total, discarded, error, GET, GETNEXT, SET, GETBULK, trap, GET variables, GETNEXT variables, SET variables, GETBULK variables, INFORM sent, INFORM re-sent, INFORM timed out, INFORM acked, INFORM REPORT

tools.name: agent.getValidate
tools.description: Is a bitmask in which with the following bits (from LSB) check for type, length, range, access

tools.name: agent.listIpaliases
tools.description: The agent host address (set with mimic agent set host) is not in this list, since it is already accessible separately with mimic agent get host.

tools.name: agent.listTimerScripts
tools.description: The command mimic timer script list lists global timer scripts, the command /mimic/timer/script/{agentNum}/list is the per-agent equivalent NOTE Global timer scripts run globally but within them you can address individual agents using {agentNum}. To schedule timerscripts for an individual agent, use /mimic/timer/script/{agentNum}.

tools.name: agent.reload
tools.description: This only works for halted agents. The net effect is the same as restarting an agent (ie. stop, start, halt), but without disconnecting the network (and thus existing connections).

tools.name: agent.setHost
tools.description: Currently, only IPv4 addresses are allowed as the main address of the agent, but both IPv4 and IPv6 addresses are allowed as IP aliases for the agent.

tools.name: agent.setInformTimeout
tools.description: The agent will retransmit INFORM PDUs at this interval until it has received a reply from the manager.

tools.name: agent.setInterface
tools.description: network interface card for the agent

tools.name: agent.setMibs
tools.description: set of MIBs, simulations and scenarios

tools.name: agent.setPdusize
tools.description: The limit for this configurable is 65536

tools.name: agent.setProtocols
tools.description: protocols supported by agent as a comma-separated list

tools.name: agent.setValidate
tools.description: Is a bitmask in which with the following bits (from LSB) check for type, length, range, access. A default value of 65535 does all validation checking.

tools.name: agent.start
tools.description: For speed, this operation will complete asynchronously. A successful return from this command means the starting of the agent is in progress. If you need to rely on the agent to have completed startup, you should wait for it's state to become RUNNING.

tools.name: agent.startIpalias
tools.description: port defaults to 161 if not specified.

tools.name: agent.statusIpalias
tools.description: port defaults to 161 if not specified.

tools.name: agent.stopIpalias
tools.description: port defaults to 161 if not specified.

tools.name: agent.trapConfigDel
tools.description: Remove a trap destination from the set of destinations.

tools.name: agent.trapConfigList
tools.description: Each trap destination is identified with an IP address and a port number. The default port number is the standard SNMP trap port 162.

tools.name: agent.trapList
tools.description: List the outstanding asynchronous traps for this agent instance.

tools.description: Statistics of fields indicated in the headers

tools.description: This list is guaranteed to be sorted into increasing order.

tools.name: daemon.getCfgfile

tools.name: daemon.getCfgFileChanged

tools.name: daemon.getChangedConfigList

tools.description: This list contains at most 5000 agent(s), and is guaranteed to be sorted into increasing order.

To schedule timerscripts for an individual agent, use /mimic/timer/script/{agentNum}.

tools.name: daemon.storePersists

tools.name: daemon.storeSave
tools.description: The MIMIC daemon caches persistent objects and their changes, and writes them to disk at program termination. If it were to crash, these changes would be lost. This operation allows to checkpoint the cache, ie. write changes to persistent objects to disk. To save the lab configuration with per-agent persistent information the mimic save operation needs to be used.

tools.name: valuespace.add
tools.description: The object needs to specify the MIB object with the INDEX clause, usually an object whose name ends with Entry.

tools.name: valuespace.evalValue
tools.description: Evaluate the values of the specified instance instance for each specified MIB object object and return it as it would through SNMP requests.

tools.name: valuespace.getInfo
tools.description: Return the syntactical information for the specified object, such as type, size, range, enumerations, and ACCESS.

tools.name: valuespace.getInstances
tools.description: This enables MIB browsing of the MIB on the current agent.

tools.name: valuespace.getMib
tools.description: This will only return a MIB name if the object is unmistakeably defined in a MIB.

tools.name: valuespace.getName
tools.description: Return the symbolic name of the specified object identifier.

tools.name: valuespace.getObjects
tools.description: This command is similar to the ls or dir operating system commands to list filesystem directories.

tools.name: valuespace.getOid
tools.description: Return the numeric OID of the specified object.

tools.name: valuespace.getState
tools.description: To disable traversal into a MIB object and any subtree underneath, set the state to 0, else set the state to 1.

tools.name: valuespace.getValue
tools.description: Get a variable in the Value Space.

tools.name: valuespace.getVariables
tools.description: This enables variable browsing of the MIB on the current agent.

tools.name: valuespace.mevalValue
tools.description: Evaluate the values of the specified instance instance for each specified MIB object object and return it as it would through SNMP requests.

tools.name: valuespace.mgetValue
tools.description: This is a performance optimization of the mimic value get command, to be used when many variables are requested.

tools.name: valuespace.msetValue
tools.description: This is a performance optimization of the mimic value set command, to be used when many variables are to be set.

tools.name: valuespace.munsetValue
tools.description: This is a performance optimization of the mimic value unset command, to be used when many variables are to be unset.

tools.name: valuespace.remove
tools.description: The object needs to specify the MIB object with the INDEX clause, usually an object whose name ends with Entry.

tools.name: valuespace.setState
tools.description: To disable traversal into a MIB object and any subtree underneath, set the state to 0, else set the state to 1.

tools.name: valuespace.setValue
tools.description: NOTE to set a binary string value, specify a string starting with \\x followed by pairs of hexadecimal digits, eg. "\\x 01 23 45". This command also assigns SNMP PDU action scripts for GET* and SET requests on a MIB object. The instance parameter must be 0. The following variables enable actions, g - The specified TCL script will be run on GET or GETNEXT requests. It has to exist under the simulation directory. s - The specified script will be run on SET requests. It has to exist under the simulation directory. This command also controls advanced trap generation functionality. The following variables control trap generation r, tu, c - These variables together represent the rate settings for the trap. r and tu is the actual per second rate and c represents the total duration in seconds for which the trap is sent. As soon as the c variable is set, the trap generation begins, for this reason it should be the last variable set for a particular trap. The following variables have to be set before setting the c variable to modify the behavior of the generated trap(s). OBJECT - An object name when used as a variable is looked up during the trap send and the value of that variable is included in the PDU. OBJECT.i - This type of variable will be used to assign an optional instance for the specified object in the traps varbind. The value of this variable identifies the index. e.g. The commands below will send ifIndex.2 with a value of 5 in the linkUp trap PDU. i - This variable is used to specify any extra version specific information to the trap generation code. Here is what it can be used to represent for various SNMP versions SNMPv1 - [community_string][,[enterprise][,agent_addr]] SNMPv2c - community_string SNMPv2 - source_party,destination_party,context SNMPv3 - user_name,context v - This variable lets the user override the version of the PDU being generated. The possible values are - "1", "2c", "2" and "3". o - This variable is used for traps that need extra variables to be added to the PDU along with the ones defined in the MIB as its variables. This lets the user force extra objects (along with instances if needed). All variables to be sent need to be assigned to the o variable. O - To omit any variables which are defined in the MIB you can use the O (capital o) variable. This needs to be set to the list of OIDs of the variable bindings in the order defined in the MIB. ip - The variable ip is used for generating the trap from the N-th IP alias address. a - This variable associates an action script to the trap or INFORM request. The action script specified in the value of this variable has to exist in the simulation directory. It will be executed before each instance of the trap is sent out. I - This optional variable controls the generation of INFORM PDUs. An INFORM is sent only if the variable is non-zero, else a TRAP is generated. R, T, E - This variable associates an action script to the INFORM request. The action script specified in the value of this variable has to exist in the simulation directory. The action script associated with the R variable will be executed on receiving a INFORM RESPONSE, the one associated with the T variable on a timeout (ie. no response), the one associated with the E variable on a report PDU. eid.IP-ADDRESS.PORT - control variable allows to configure message authoritative engine id for the destination specified by IP-ADDRESS and optionally by PORT. eb.IP-ADDRESS.PORT - control variable allows to configure message authoritative engine boots. et.IP-ADDRESS.PORT - control variable allows to configure message authoritative engine time.

tools.name: valuespace.splitOid
tools.description: This is useful if you have an OID which is a combination of object and instance.

tools.name: valuespace.unsetValue
